title: Llama7B 131k attention/KV spill scheduler
hypothesis: The llama7b_proxy 131k-token 400 mm2 point remains memory dominated because the HBM-spilled fraction cannot be hidden without sufficient prefetch distance, HBM request parallelism, and NoC/shared-SRAM service efficiency.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How much of the llama7b_proxy 131k spill latency can be hidden by tile-level prefetch scheduling and finite HBM parallelism?
include: capacity/NoC baseline residency split for shared SRAM plus HBM spill
prefetch starting after QKV production or during QKV production

expected_benefit: identifies whether the hard llama7B point is HBM-request limited, NoC limited, or tile-compute limited
shows whether producer-prefetch overlap has enough room to recover the spill-heavy case
selects the next RTL-facing shared-SRAM/NoC scheduler or HBM interface target
risks: prefetching assumes deterministic KV addresses and sufficient buffering
HBM efficiency is still an input parameter rather than a measured controller result
the scheduler model serializes tile compute and should be validated later with a fuller trace

required_evaluations.objective: Run a tile-level spill scheduler for the llama7b_proxy 131k 400 mm2 attention/KV point.

expected_result.reason: Use tile-level spill scheduling to choose the next concrete memory/NoC RTL or controller measurement.
